response.justification: The candidate has genuine engineering breadth spanning ~20 years and shows recent involvement in AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, but falls significantly short of the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) profile. The role demands director-level strategic influence, recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products at scale, and deep cloud fluency — none of which are evidenced here. AI involvement is limited to roughly one month at the current role, there is no advanced degree in an AI-related field, and the resume reflects a hands-on individual contributor trajectory rather than strategic advisory or thought-leadership positioning.
strengths: Approximately 20 years of diverse engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, satisfying the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform serving real-world NGO use cases
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling at re:cinq, demonstrating willingness to engage with emerging AI workflows
concerns: AI expertise is nascent — limited to the current role started April 2025 (~1 month at time of resume), with no prior ML/AI product experience or recognized contributions in the field
No cloud architecture depth; AWS CDK is mentioned in passing within a pricing-team context, far below the cloud platform strategy competency the role requires

No evidence of strategic customer advisory work, director-level scope, or external thought leadership in AI — the candidate's trajectory is that of a senior hands-on engineer, not a principal strategist
